diagrams.title: Minimax Algorithm Game Tree
diagrams.description: A complete minimax game tree showing MAX and MIN players at alternating levels, with leaf node utility values propagating upward to determine the optimal move for the root node.
elements: Leaf nodes with numeric utility values (0-10)
Upward arrows showing backpropagation of minimax values
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental minimax algorithm structure where MAX tries to maximize and MIN tries to minimize, showing how values propagate from leaves to the root.

diagrams.title: Alpha-Beta Pruning Example
diagrams.description: A game tree demonstrating alpha-beta pruning with pruned branches crossed out, showing how unnecessary exploration is eliminated while finding the same optimal result as full minimax.
elements: Game tree with MAX root node
Legend showing α = best value for MAX, β = best value for MIN
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates how alpha-beta pruning reduces search space by eliminating branches that cannot influence the final decision, maintaining correctness while improving efficiency.

diagrams.title: Alpha Beta Value Propagation
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of alpha and beta values being updated during depth-first traversal, showing the pruning condition (β ≤ α) and how values propagate between levels.
elements: Node labels showing current α and β values
Timeline or sequence showing exploration order
Comparison of pruned subtree to original
diagrams.purpose: Clarifies the mechanics of alpha-beta pruning by showing exactly how alpha increases at MAX nodes and beta decreases at MIN nodes, and when pruning occurs.
